nameMeanings.name: Waltham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from Waltham (Lincs, Essex, Leics) or any of the numerous other places so named.

variants.name: Whatham
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Waltham  .

variants.name: Wattam
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Waltham  .

variants.name: Whattam
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Waltham  .

nameMeanings.name: Humphrey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Norman name, Hunfrid, of Germanic origin, derived from hun ‘bear-cub, warrior’ + fred, frid ‘peace’. The Norman form absorbed the native Old English form, Hunfrith, which existed in England before the Conquest. The spelling with -ph- reflects classicizing influence. Since the 16th century it has enjoyed a modest popularity in England, having been borne most notably by the youngest son of King Henry IV , the Duke of Gloucester ( 1391–1447 ), known as ‘Duke Humphrey’. He was noted as a patron of literature, and founded what became the Bodleian Library at Oxford. In modern times, probably the most famous bearer has been the film star Humphrey Bogart ( 1899–1957 ).

en.lifeSummary: Humphrey Waltham was born in 1377, in Great Waltham, Essex, England, United Kingdom. He had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Agatha. He died in 1445, in his hometown, at the age of 68.

ko.lifeSummary: Humphrey Waltham(은)는 1377년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 그리트 월섬에서 태어났다. 그는 Agatha(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1445년 그의 고향에서 68세의 나이로 사망했다.
